summary: An exact relational join must account for every box and locate every potentially referenced chest, giving a linear input-inspection lower bound; one hash join and aggregate scan match it.

optimality.argument: In the general unordered-table access model, changing any uninspected box alters a direct count or reference, and a matching chest record may occur at any uninspected chest row. Thus all B+C=R input rows may need inspection. Building a chest hash table and scanning every box performs O(1) expected join and aggregation work per row, for O(R) total time.

replacement_checks.evidence: An adversary can change fruit totals through the final uninspected box or place a referenced chest at the final uninspected chest row.
One hash build over Chests followed by one left-join aggregation scan over Boxes performs constant expected work per input row.
Authored fixtures cover the public sample, null chest IDs, one joined chest, repeated chest references, zero fruit counts, and an unreferenced chest.
